seoTitle: How to Add a Custom Domain on Gumroad
metaDescription: Connect your own domain name to Gumroad by updating DNS records, verifying ownership, and activating the domain for a professional store URL.
intro: Using your own domain instead of the default Gumroad URL makes your storefront look more professional and gives you a memorable link to promote. This guide covers configuring the DNS records at your domain provider, connecting the domain in Gumroad, and verifying it's active.

steps.heading: Open domain settings in Gumroad
steps.body: Navigate to your Gumroad account's domain configuration section.

steps.heading: Configure DNS records
steps.body: Update the DNS records at your domain registrar as instructed by Gumroad so it points to your storefront.

steps.heading: Connect and verify the domain
steps.body: Enter your domain in Gumroad and complete the verification step to confirm ownership.

steps.heading: Test the custom domain setup
steps.body: Visit your custom domain in a browser to confirm it correctly loads your Gumroad storefront.

steps.heading: Troubleshoot common errors
steps.body: If verification fails, double-check that DNS records match exactly what Gumroad requested, since propagation delays or typos are common causes.

faq.question: Do I need to buy a domain separately to use this feature?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to own a domain name through a separate registrar before you can connect it to your Gumroad store.

faq.question: How long does it take for a custom domain to activate?
faq.answer: DNS changes can take anywhere from a few minutes to 24-48 hours to fully propagate before verification succeeds.

faq.question: What's the most common reason a custom domain fails to verify?
faq.answer: Incorrectly entered DNS records or not waiting long enough for propagation are the most frequent causes of verification failures.

faq.question: Can I switch back to the default Gumroad URL later?
faq.answer: Yes, connecting a custom domain doesn't permanently remove your default Gumroad URL — you can adjust your domain settings again if needed.
